# Transcrição: 3. Calls not on a Regular Basis

- **Fonte:** 3. Calls not on a Regular Basis
- **Duração:** 01:42
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:42]

Your calls need to be on a regular basis. The randomity, one call now, 30 days later, another call, three months from now to be another one. They're not regular enough. You need them frequent. You need volume and you need frequency. The third biggest mistake with follow-up is not regular enough. You need a regular...it's like working out. You could work out once a week, nothing's going to change. You are just stay home and eat cookies. It's not even an attempt to change things. Don't even pat yourself on the back for one workout. The only thing that happens is you hurt once a week. Four times a month you're in pain. The way for that workout to work is every day. You're going to feel better about yourself. It's going to create a discipline. It's going to create a different sense, self-esteem of yourself. The calls, the follow-up, the mail needs to be on a regular basis. Remember with follow-up, be the stream, not the rock. The rock will not beat the stream ever. You need to be consistent and regular there all the time because when I start calling Pat people back with regularity over and over today, three days from now, six days from now, we're going to show you a whole schedule that we lay out for you over a period of two years of follow-up. Lay it out. By that time you'll pick up the rest of it. Calls on a regular basis will start to wear a client out. It will start to build your persona in the marketplace. It will make you the celebrity. It will take you out of obscurity because you're regular. You're there all the time. You're like the policeman on the corner, the teacher in the classroom, you're dependable, you're trustworthy, you're the relationship, you're the contact to become a contract. Be regular. It's a mistake in follow-up.
